passage: A state energy commission tracked the share of electricity generated from renewable sources in four states from 2020 to 2023. The commission's report highlighted that State D more than doubled its renewable energy share during this period.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the commission's claim about State D?

choices.label: A
choices.text: State D's renewable energy share rose from 12% in 2020 to 28% in 2023, representing more than a twofold increase.

choices.label: B
choices.text: State A's renewable energy share increased from 22% in 2020 to 31% in 2023, a gain of 9 percentage points.

choices.label: C
choices.text: State D had the lowest renewable energy share among all four states in 2020 at 12%, while State C led at 35%.

choices.label: D
choices.text: By 2023, State C had a renewable energy share of 41%, which was higher than State D's 28% that same year.

explanation: The claim is that State D more than doubled its renewable energy share from 2020 to 2023. Choice A directly supports this by showing State D went from 12% to 28%, which is more than double (12% x 2 = 24%, and 28% > 24% by a comfortable margin). Choice B discusses State A. Choice C mentions State D's 2020 value but doesn't show the growth to demonstrate doubling. Choice D compares State C to State D in 2023 only.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: cites State D's 12% in 2020 and 28% in 2023, confirming more than a twofold increase (28/12 = 2.33x).
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (irrelevant data): cites State A's figures, which are unrelated to the claim about State D.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (incomplete evidence): mentions State D's 2020 starting point but does not cite its 2023 value, so the reader cannot verify the doubling claim.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (wrong comparison): compares State C to State D in a single year rather than showing State D's growth over time.

imageDescription: Data table titled 'Renewable Energy Share by State (% of Total Electricity Generation).' Columns: State, 2020, 2023. Rows: State A - 22% - 31%; State B - 18% - 24%; State C - 35% - 41%; State D - 12% - 28%. Source: State Energy Commission, 2024.

chartData.title: Renewable Energy Share by State (% of Total Electricity Generation)
